block_01: Working as an affiliate can be hard and stressful. Balancing your work and personal life can make you want to put off some decisions or tasks for just a little bit. After all, what’s the harm in a little distraction? And then, suddenly, it is 2 AM and you need to finish ten tasks before sunrise!
block_02: Almost everyone has fallen into that trap at least once. It is especially easy to do if you are working remotely, as there are no fixed hours, bosses to tell you to get to work, and a lot of distractions. To help you avoid it, we’ve decided to try out a new topic that we thought would be useful for you: that of organizing your work process. There is a shortlist of a few tips that would help you to work efficiently and not to fall prey to the dreaded beast that is procrastination.
block_04: Being able to work everywhere is an important part of working remotely, sure. However, there is a reason why office format of work, well, works. It motivates people, helps them to get into the right mindset. It is easy to slack off or overwork yourself without a timetable to keep. To keep your mind in a game, you will need to set up a workspace, where you can sit down and do your tasks, and where you can leave your work and be able to relax afterward. It could be a coworking space, a spare room modeled into an office, or even a table in a corner - just make sure that there is somewhere you can take your work to.
block_06: Motivation, despite being essential to your work, can be rather fragile. To ensure that you always have the drive to continue with your task, you will need to find something to inspire you. But don’t worry, we’ve got you. Your personal manager will always help if you find yourself in a bind, or in need of advice. Another way to get inspired would be reading our newsletters, new and old, as they are very informative, trust us. After all, we are the ones who wrote them. With the right motivation and information, even the hardest work won’t look that scary.
block_08: Prioritizing your tasks and goals can really help you make your work process more efficient. That way, more urgent tasks will be completed on time, you won’t stress out over less important things, and your efficiency will rise significantly.
block_10: can help to decide on your priorities. You can divide your tasks by their importance and urgency so that you can delegate or postpone less important and not urgent things.
block_12: Many remote workers at first feel that scheduling is outdated and should remain in stuffy offices. That couldn’t be any farther from the truth. As it was said, procrastination is a dreadful beast that will get you if your focus and motivation waver. Setting up a schedule can help you in preventing that. Besides, scheduling doesn’t mean that you can’t be flexible anymore. After all, you can still work at any time you want, you just need to make sure that you ARE working during that time.
block_13: Find your peak hours and use them
block_14: Different people have different biological clocks. Some are early risers who awake fresh as daisies and are ready for work at 7 AM but get sluggish and tired the closer it is to evening. Others only survive mornings through the liberal application of coffee and energy drinks but are thrumming with energy in the afternoon. Test yourself to see when you can work at 100%, and plan your work time accordingly.
block_16: Sometimes even your favorite work can become a chore and demotivate you. On days like this it is easy to postpone it, and just slack off. Remote workers are hit harder since there is no one around to help out with their rut. It is also easy to get overlooked, as you are not in the office to remind everyone about your achievements. So you should document your actions, goals, and results. That way you can motivate yourself, fix any problems that you encounter, and show your progress if you need to.
block_17: Procrastination, demotivation, and low self-discipline will be your main enemies on a path to work efficiently and productively, but we hope that our tips will help you overcome that fearsome trio.
